references.title: Evaluating Learning Style-Based Grouping Strategies in Real-World Collaborative Learning Environment
references.journal: Distributed, Ambient and Pervasive Interactions: Technologies and Contexts

annotations.text: Group composition is a problem that is widely discussed in collaborative learning scenarios. The authors compare different approaches to grouping students in a face-to-face scenario. Grouping is done on an ontology-base and options are homogenous, heterogenous and random. Authors measure the activity levels and have students peer-review their work.
